# CoFirstTimeBuyerGrants

> Colorado first-time homebuyer grant and assistance programs — bilingual (English and Spanish).

CoFirstTimeBuyerGrants connects Colorado first-time home buyers with grant programs and down payment assistance that can provide thousands of dollars toward a home purchase — money that does not need to be repaid. The site is operated by Pivot Lending Group, a licensed Colorado mortgage company.

## Pages

- [Home (English)](https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com/en): Overview of first-time buyer programs in Colorado
- [Home (Spanish)](https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com/es): Versión en español
- [Counties (English)](https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com/en/counties): Program availability by Colorado county
- [Learn (English)](https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com/en/learn): Educational articles for first-time buyers
- [FAQ (English)](https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com/en/faq): Frequently asked questions
- [Contact](https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com/en/contact): Get a free consultation

## Service

CoFirstTimeBuyerGrants helps Colorado residents who are buying a home for the first time (or who have not owned a home in the past three years) find and apply for:
- Down payment assistance programs
- Closing cost assistance programs
- Grant-style funding that does not need to be repaid

Eligibility depends on income, credit score, purchase price, and county. The service is free — buyers are connected with a licensed Colorado mortgage professional at no cost and with no obligation.

## Coverage

- 9 Colorado counties covered
- Bilingual service: English and Spanish
- Area served: Colorado, United States

## Key Facts

- First-time homebuyer definition: no primary residence ownership in the past 3 years
- Down payment requirements: 3–20% conventional, 3.5% FHA, 0% VA/USDA
- Credit score requirements: 620+ conventional, 580+ FHA (varies by program)
- Closing costs typically 2–5% of loan amount; assistance programs may help
- Grant funds used for primary residence purchase are generally not taxable income
